should_trigger.prompt: Turn these requirements into a conceptual graph and give me CGIF: every purchase order over 5000 dollars must be approved by a manager.
should_trigger.why: Requires conceptual graph modeling, quantifier scope, relation signatures, and CGIF serialization.

should_trigger.prompt: I have two knowledge graph fragments. Use Sowa conceptual graph operations to tell whether one is a specialization of the other.
should_trigger.why: Directly asks for canonical CG operations such as restrict, join, unrestrict, and detach.

should_trigger.prompt: Map this sentence to predicate calculus using conceptual graphs: Tom believes that Mary wants to buy a car.
should_trigger.why: Requires nested contexts and logical mapping.

should_trigger.prompt: Audit this CGIF for broken coreference labels and invalid relation arity: [Go *x] (Agnt ?x [Person: John]) (Dest ?y [City: Boston]).
should_trigger.why: Requires CGIF conformance and modeling checks.

should_trigger.prompt: Design a Common Logic friendly interchange representation for a small ontology of events, agents, destinations, and instruments.
should_trigger.why: Requires the CGIF/Common Logic representation ladder and relation signature discipline.

should_trigger.prompt: Without looking anything up, model this policy as a Sowa conceptual graph, give CGIF, and list relation signatures: every employee who approves an invoice must be different from the employee who submitted it.
should_trigger.why: Verifies standalone execution for concepts, identity, inequality, quantifier scope, relation signatures, and CGIF.

should_trigger.prompt: Use canonical conceptual graph operations to compare A: [Animal *x] (Attr ?x [Brown]) with B: [Dog *x] (Attr ?x [Brown]) and explain the implication direction.
should_trigger.why: Requires restrict-by-type classification and the specialization/generalization direction without consulting the book.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Summarize the history of semantic networks in AI.
should_not_trigger.why: Background-only request; no CG modeling, operations, mapping, or audit task.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Draw a pretty node-link diagram for our product roadmap.
should_not_trigger.why: Visual graph styling without logical semantics or conceptual graph constraints.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Find me the latest ISO price for Common Logic standards.
should_not_trigger.why: Current-price lookup, not conceptual graph execution.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Convert this JSON object to a Mermaid flowchart.
should_not_trigger.why: Diagram conversion only; no CG type/referent/context/logical mapping requirements.
Historical explanation, not Sowa-style CG practice.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Create a Neo4j property graph schema and Cypher indexes for customers and orders.
should_not_trigger.why: Graph database implementation request without formal CG semantics, CGIF, Common Logic, or canonical operations.

should_not_trigger.prompt: Make this graph look cleaner by choosing colors, icons, and edge thickness.
should_not_trigger.why: Presentation styling only; no conceptual graph modeling or logical commitments.

edge_cases.prompt: Represent this as a knowledge graph, but I do not care about formal logic.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Do not force full CGIF. Offer a lightweight graph unless the user needs logical mapping, scope, or interchange checks.

edge_cases.prompt: Use CGIF for a modal belief statement, but keep it ISO-standard only.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Trigger the skill, then warn that rich Proposition/Situation contexts are beyond core ISO CGIF/Common Logic and may require an extension such as IKL-style treatment.

edge_cases.prompt: Check whether my RDF triples can be translated to conceptual graphs.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Trigger if the user wants semantic mapping; distinguish RDF/OWL subset translation from full Common Logic expressiveness.

edge_cases.prompt: I need to merge two entity graphs where the same name might not mean the same individual.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Trigger for coreference and join/detach audit; avoid joining concepts unless identity is explicitly supported.

edge_cases.prompt: Generate Cypher queries from this domain model.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only trigger if the user also asks for logical commitments, CGIF, formal graph operations, or conceptual graph validation.

edge_cases.prompt: Audit this CGIF and repair it if possible: [Go *x] (Agnt ?x [Person: John]) (Dest ?y [City: Boston])
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Trigger; identify unresolved ?y, explain the semantic defect, and repair by binding Dest to ?x or by defining the intended Go event explicitly.

edge_cases.prompt: Translate 'Every manager approves some invoice' to CGIF, but show whether 'some invoice' is inside or outside the universal scope.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Trigger; make quantifier scope explicit, mention alternative readings if ambiguous, and avoid presenting a hidden existential as unscoped.

edge_cases.prompt: Compare two graphs inside a negated context where one restricts Animal to Dog.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Trigger; apply canonical operations and note that specialization/generalization implication direction reverses in negative contexts.

edge_cases.prompt: Represent Alice believes Bob owns a car in ISO-only CGIF and do not use any extensions.
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Trigger; separate outer assertion from belief context, then warn if the requested modality/metalevel treatment exceeds core ISO/Common Logic expressiveness.

must_include_for_should_trigger: trigger verdict and selected artifact contract
proposition extraction with polarity and scope
concept nodes with type and referent decisions
conceptual relations with valence, ordered arguments, and local signatures
coreference, identity, context, and quantifier-scope decisions
requested CGIF, logic mapping, comparison, or audit artifact
limitations for approximate, extended, or unsupported Common Logic features
must_include_for_should_not_trigger: reason conceptual graph semantics are not needed
lighter alternative such as generic diagramming, graph database query, bibliography lookup, or prose summary
must_include_for_edge_cases: gate that is ambiguous or failing
smallest clarifying question or explicit assumption
repair path for labels, arity, scope, identity, or target notation
whether the result is equivalent, more specific, more general, changed commitment, or incomparable
must_not_do: tell the user to read the original Sowa pages before proceeding
emit CGIF without relation signatures or scope caveats
promise byte-for-byte round-trip equivalence across notations
